query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Lee Jae-myung's approval ratings in late June/early July 2026:

**Bottom line:** Approval fell sharply from spring highs (~64-66%) to below-50% troughs in late June, then **modestly rebounded** in early July — but a Realmeter "dead cross" remained in effect.

**Gallup Korea:**
- Hit a **record-low 51%** (survey June 24-26)
- Rebounded **+3pp to 54%** (survey June 30-July 2; disapproval 36%), credited to "three mega regional-investment projects"
- **No dead cross** — approval stayed above disapproval

**Realmeter:**
- **First dead cross since inauguration** appeared June 22 release (approval 46.7% vs. disapproval 49.7%)
- Fell for **6 consecutive weeks** from a mid-May peak of 60.5%
- July 6 release: slight rebound to 47.0% approval, but disapproval 49.2% — **dead cross persisted for a 3rd straight week**

**Is it still falling?** The decline paused/reversed by early July in both series, but off multi-month lows and Realmeter still shows net-negative.

**Drivers of decline:** (1) June 3 local-election fallout and accountability blame, (2) Election Commission ballot-mismanagement controversy, (3) ruling Democratic Party internal conflict ahead of its Aug 17 convention, (4) economic anxiety (stock plunge, high won-dollar rate, welfare-spending concerns), (5) sharp erosion among 50s-age voters (-9.1pp in one week) and conservative regions.

**Dead cross:** YES in Realmeter (and separately in KSOI); NO in Gallup.

sources.title: Opinion polling on the Lee Jae Myung presidency
sources.snippet: Lee Jae Myung inaugurated 4 June 2025; aggregates opinion polling on his presidency.

key_facts: Gallup Korea: Lee's approval hit a record-low 51% in the June 24-26, 2026 survey (released June 26) — lowest since his June 2025 inauguration.
Gallup Korea: approval rebounded +3pp to 54% (disapproval 36%) in the June 30-July 2 survey released July 3, n=1,005; credited to 'three mega regional-investment projects.'
Gallup Korea did NOT record a dead cross; approval stayed above disapproval even at the 51% low.
Realmeter: FIRST dead cross since inauguration appeared in the June 22 release (survey June 15-19): approval 46.7% vs disapproval 49.7%; approval -4.8pp, disapproval +5.5pp.
Realmeter: June 29 release (survey June 22-26) showed approval 46.5% / disapproval 49.5% — sixth consecutive weekly decline.
Realmeter: July 6 release (survey June 29-July 3, n=2,525) approval rebounded to 47.0% (+0.5pp), disapproval 49.2% — dead cross persisted for a third straight week.
Realmeter approval fell from a mid-May peak of 60.5% across six consecutive weekly declines.

Decline drivers: June 3 local-election fallout/accountability blame, Election Commission ballot-mismanagement controversy, ruling Democratic Party internal conflict ahead of Aug 17 convention, and economic anxiety (stock plunge, high won-dollar rate, welfare-spending concerns).
Realmeter June 22 poll showed core-group erosion: voters in their 50s -9.1pp in one week; Daegu/Gyeongbuk -9.9pp, Incheon/Gyeonggi -7.6pp, Seoul -7.4pp.
Party support (Realmeter July 6): Democratic Party 43.0% vs People Power Party 40.3%; earlier post-election Realmeter had PPP (44.3%) briefly overtaking DP.
Separate pollster KSOI also recorded a brief dead cross (approval 47.7% vs disapproval 48.2%) in late June, then rebounded to 54.7% approval in its July 6-7 survey.
By early July 2026, the decline paused/modestly reversed in both Gallup and Realmeter, but Realmeter's dead cross (disapproval > approval) remained in effect.

data_points.metric: Realmeter approval (survey June 15-19, first dead cross)
Realmeter People Power Party support (July 6 release)
Realmeter approval drop among voters in their 50s (one week, June 22 release)
